description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a boxy, rectangular form and a prominent woodgrain-patterned bezel framing the screen. The front features a convex glass display, two large rotary dials for channel and volume control, and a grid of tactile push buttons, all set within a metallic and faux-wood panel. Two thin metal antennae extend from the top left corner, characteristic of older analog TV models. The sides and back are plain, metallic gray, with no visible vents or ports, giving it a utilitarian appearance. The overall build suggests a heavy, robust construction typical of mid-to-late 20th-century televisions. The design is utilitarian, with a focus on function and durability, making it a recognizable piece of retro urban decor.
description: A vintage box-shaped television with a woodgrain bezel, convex glass screen, rotary dials, push buttons, and metal antennae. The sides and back are plain metallic gray, emphasizing its retro, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the woodgrain bezel, convex glass screen, two rotary dials, a grid of push buttons, and the metal antennae extending from the top.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain metallic gray surface and the protruding antennae, with no visible controls or screen.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a flat, featureless metallic gray back panel, with the antennae visible at the top left.
description_view_3: Right side view with a plain metallic gray surface and the antennae visible, mirroring the left side with no controls or screen.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick woodgrain-patterned bezel with metallic trim, rectangular, and visually prominent.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of two rotary dials and a grid of push buttons on the front right panel.
